strategy.principle: no phase advancement without minimal reliability guarantees

milestones.name: Detection Reliability
milestones.exit_gate: Detection outputs are explainable and never misleading

milestones.name: Extraction Resilience
milestones.goal: Extract maximum usable data from corrupted inputs
milestones.exit_gate: Extraction never crashes on recoverable errors

milestones.name: Full Transparency (Reporting)
milestones.goal: Expose complete system state to the user
milestones.exit_gate: User can fully understand what happened

milestones.name: System Destruction (Adversarial Testing)
milestones.goal: Break the system to reveal hidden flaws

milestones.name: Transformation Stage (pure, in-memory)
milestones.goal: Normalise extracted DrawerRecords into a typed bundle ready for ChromaDB 1.x ingestion. Pure function: no I/O, no writes, no network, no chromadb import.
success_criteria: TransformedBundle dataclass produced and stored on ctx.transformed_data
Per-drawer normalisation rejects unsupported metadata value types (lists, dicts, None) with structured anomalies
Integrity analysis surfaces duplicate ids, blank ids, empty/non-string documents, structural metadata issues as anomalies (NOT raised)
step_transform replaces the NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub anomaly with real work
report_builder.py 'transformation' section returns a structured summary instead of None
Transformation never writes anywhere; pure function semantics enforced by test (no chromadb import in transformation/)
failure_modes_to_eliminate: Hidden record drops without per-drawer anomaly
Module-level chromadb import (transformation must not depend on the writer)
milestones.exit_gate: step_transform produces a TransformedBundle deterministically, every drop or coercion is structurally represented as an anomaly, and no writer dependency leaks into the transformation module.

milestones.name: Reconstruction Stage (the only writer)
milestones.goal: Build a fresh ChromaDB 1.5.7 palace at ctx.target_path from ctx.transformed_data via the public chromadb client API. Strictly atomic: on any failure, target_path is left in the same state as before the run (rolled back).
success_criteria: chromadb>=1.5.7,<2 added to pyproject.toml dependencies; version pin matches the supported pair documented in README
target_path safety check refuses non-empty existing directories before any write
ReconstructionResult dataclass stored on ctx.reconstruction_result (collection_name, imported_count, target_manifest_path, chromadb_version)
Batch insert (size 500, matching prototype) via collection.add(); per-batch failure raises ReconstructionError with structured anomaly listing affected drawer ids
Atomicity: any failure after target_path.mkdir cleans up the partial directory; tested by an injected mid-batch failure fixture
target_manifest written at TARGET_MANIFEST_FILENAME with bundle provenance (source palace path, detected_format, drawer_count, chromadb_version, mempalace_version)
step_reconstruct replaces the NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub anomaly with real work; exit code 5 reachable on reconstruction failure (already pinned by M6.4)
New CLI subcommand `migrate` requires --target; `inspect` and `analyze` continue to refuse --target (their pipelines do not include reconstruction)
report_builder.py 'reconstruction' section returns a structured summary instead of None
failure_modes_to_eliminate: Partial target palace left on disk after failure
Silent overwrite of an existing populated target directory
Source palace ever opened in write mode (source remains read-only at the SQLite URI level)
Reconstruction triggered without an explicit --target (the analyze/inspect pipelines must remain non-destructive)
milestones.exit_gate: A fresh ChromaDB 1.5.7 palace is built atomically at --target, the source is byte-identical before and after the run (verified by test), and any failure leaves no partial target on disk.

milestones.name: Target Parity Validation
milestones.goal: Once reconstruction lands, lift the two parity checks currently parked in checks_not_performed (target_record_count_parity, target_id_set_parity) into real checks, and add document/metadata content parity. Validation never opens the target in write mode.
success_criteria: target_record_count_parity check moved out of _SKIPPED_RECONSTRUCTION when ctx.reconstruction_result is non-None; reads count from a read-only chromadb client
target_id_set_parity check moved out of _SKIPPED_RECONSTRUCTION; missing_in_target and unexpected_in_target sets surfaced via structured anomaly evidence
target_document_hash_parity check (sha256 over normalised text) surfaces content mismatches per drawer id
target_metadata_parity check surfaces metadata key/value divergence per drawer id

target_embedding_presence check (best-effort, capped severity MEDIUM): if include=['embeddings'] is accessible, missing-embedding ids are surfaced; if not accessible, check status is inconclusive with reason
checks_not_performed list shrinks accordingly; report_builder.EXPLICITLY_NOT_CHECKED loses the two parity entries
All target-side reads use chromadb.PersistentClient with no writes; tested by source/target mtime invariant
failure_modes_to_eliminate: False target validation (silently passing when target is empty or partially built)
Target opened in write mode by validation
checks_not_performed retaining target_*_parity entries after reconstruction is implemented (regression of M5.5 honesty contract)
milestones.exit_gate: For every successful migrate run, the target palace is structurally and content-wise identical to the transformed bundle, and any divergence is surfaced as a structured failed/inconclusive validation check.

milestones.name: Write-Path Adversarial + Hardening
milestones.goal: Extend the M7 adversarial corpus and the M8 baseline envelope to cover the new transform/reconstruct/parity stages. No new pipeline stages, no new exit codes, no new CLI flags introduced by M12.
success_criteria: Adversarial fixtures: existing target directory non-empty, target path is a file, target on read-only filesystem, mid-batch chromadb failure (injected), transformation rejects-all-drawers input, source-mtime-invariant violated

All M7 cross-cutting invariants (no exit 10, no traceback w/o --debug, no silent CRITICAL, schema_version stable, JSON-safe, anomaly well-formed, forbidden-vocabulary, stage attribution, stdout/stderr discipline) re-verified on every new fixture
Baseline corpus extended with at least one successful migrate run; runtime_envelope.json gains a new entry for the migrate command
Stability invariants: report-signature stability re-asserted on a successful migrate run (modulo run_id/started_at/imported_count fields if non-deterministic)
Logging discipline AST walk re-checks transformation/ and reconstruction/ for forbidden call sites
failure_modes_to_eliminate: Reconstruction crash that escapes as exit 10 instead of exit 5
Silent partial-target left on disk after adversarial failure injection
New code paths in transformation/ or reconstruction/ slipping in print/logging without M8.1 catching it
milestones.exit_gate: The full M7 invariant battery still holds across the extended corpus, the M8 baseline includes a migrate-success entry, and the write path fails loudly and atomically on every adversarial fixture.

non_goals: Retrieval-parity validation (record-retrieval / compare-retrieval from the prototype) — out of scope; belongs to a separate post-migration audit tool
Usage-scenario-parity validation (record-usage / compare-usage from the prototype) — out of scope; belongs to a separate post-migration audit tool
MCP-runtime validation (validate-mcp-runtime from the prototype) — out of scope; the migrator does not own the runtime
Embedding re-computation or embedding-shape validation — embeddings are passed through opaquely; chromadb 1.x re-derives them from documents on insert
Multi-collection palaces — single-collection invariant is preserved (M2 already enforces it on the source side)
Migration of palaces other than chroma_0_6 — only the documented version pair is supported; other formats remain rejected at detection

current_position.next_target_reason: M9 Transformation Stage complete (672 tests green). step_transform produces a typed TransformedBundle via pure in-memory normalisation. step_reconstruct still emits NOT_IMPLEMENTED — the only remaining pipeline stub. M10 must implement the ChromaDB 1.x writer: consume TransformedBundle, open the target collection, ingest all drawers, emit structured anomalies for write failures.
current_position.description: Final Hardening (M8) exit gate satisfied. tests/hardening/ fully implemented with 4 sub-task files (11.1–11.4), shared invariant module tests/adversarial/_invariants.py, rebaseline script, and committed baseline files. Baseline files include schema_version, recorded_on, python_version, and tolerance envelope per §3.3. Tolerance is read from the baseline file by test files (no hardcoded factors). Full suite green at 718 passed.
current_position.reason: M8 exit gate conditions met: (1) tests/hardening/ exists with all four sub-task files; (2) baselines/runtime_envelope.json and baselines/report_signatures.json committed with schema_version=1, recorded_on (UTC ISO), python_version="3.12", and tolerance block (wall_clock_pct=50, rss_bytes_pct=25) per §3.3 — provenance link to HEAD is explicit; (3) 11 baseline corpus entries (allowed_exit_codes ⊆ {0, 8}) covered by all nine M7 invariants; (4) AST walk finds zero forbidden call sites in src/; (5) tolerance read from baseline file in both test_performance_baseline.py and test_memory_envelope.py — re-baseline is the only way to change tolerance; (6) no invariant xfailed/skipped without asserted reason; (7) full suite green at 718 passed; (8) TODO.json phase 11 sub-tasks 11.1–11.4 are done.
caveats: Detection still uses its own pre-M3 Evidence/Contradiction model; pipeline gate failures are mirrored into ctx.anomalies as critical anomalies but the two structured models are not unified.
Legacy add_anomaly(stage=, context=) shape is still accepted as a transitional escape hatch (still produces structurally-valid Anomalies).
transformation/reconstruction report sections remain null (stubs); stages section correctly marks them skipped or not_run.

Reconstruction-parity validation checks (target_record_count_parity, target_id_set_parity) are explicitly listed in checks_not_performed with reason 'stage_not_implemented' until reconstruction lands.
inspect exits 0 when transform/reconstruct stubs run; the --help warning and not_implemented anomalies in the report are the only signals that two stages were skipped.

CliRunner-based tests cannot assert stdout/stderr separation under Click 8.2+; those assertions are covered by subprocess invocations instead. M8.1, M8.2, M8.3, M8.4 inherit this rule (subprocess only, never CliRunner).

M7 finding (deferred, not blocking): extraction emits one aggregate DUPLICATE_EMBEDDING_IDS anomaly but writes individual FailedRow entries per duplicate row, leaving validation.consistency.failed_row_has_anomaly to flag the gap. The validation check correctly catches this — the structured surface is honest — but the per-row anomaly granularity could be tightened. Not in M7 scope.

M7 finding (deferred, not blocking): detection's structural pre-scan catches most broken-SQLite cases (sqlite_missing, zeroed_header, required_table_missing, multiple_collections, unexpected_collection_name) before extraction's own pre-flight runs. This is correct early-rejection behavior but means several extraction-stage error codes (sqlite_open_failed, sqlite_master_unreadable, no_collection, multiple_collections, unexpected_collection_name) are reachable only via inputs that bypass detection — currently no such input exists in the corpus.

M7 finding (deferred per design §6, out of scope): the §4 row 10.5 fixture list mentions symlink-loop, non-UTF-8 path component, document of size 32 MiB, embedding of wrong dimensionality, and NaN in embedding. None of these are built by the corpus today: (a) symlink-loop is not platform-portable across the project's test matrix without skipif gating that the design doc forbids; (b) non-UTF-8 path components on POSIX would require os.fsencode hackery that masks the real failure mode (OS-level filename decode); (c) oversized / NaN / wrong-dim embedding cases would require schema awareness the extractor does not currently have (it stores embeddings opaquely as BLOBs and never decodes them — there is no embedding-shape check to break). Adding these would require new pipeline behaviour (embedding decoding/validation) which is explicitly forbidden by M7 non-goal #1. Recorded here per design §6 triage.

M7 finding (deferred per design §6, out of scope): exit 8 (silent-CRITICAL guard) is enforced negatively by invariant 3 ("exit 0 ⇒ top_severity != critical") on every corpus entry, but no current production code path emits a CRITICAL anomaly without also raising MigratorError. Positive coverage of EXIT_CRITICAL_ANOMALY therefore lives in the M5/M6 unit tests of _decide_exit_code (tests/test_cli.py) and a CliRunner-level test, not in the adversarial corpus. Adding an adversarial path that emits CRITICAL-without-raise would require a new production code path which is forbidden by M7 non-goal #1 and remains forbidden by M8 non-goal #1.

M8 design caveat (recorded per M8_HARDENING_DESIGN.md §4.3 open question): whether a 'largest reasonable Chroma 0.6.x palace' fixture needs to be added specifically for memory-envelope measurements is deferred to M8.3 implementation. Default plan: use existing fixtures, scale row count via the existing build_minimal_valid_chroma_06 builder if RSS measurements prove too small to be meaningful. No new builder family is permitted.

M8 design caveat (recorded per M8_HARDENING_DESIGN.md §3.1 and §3.3): the M8 baseline files (baselines/runtime_envelope.json, baselines/report_signatures.json) are committed and regenerated only via tests/hardening/rebaseline.py in a reviewed PR. There is intentionally no --update-baseline flag on the test runner; drift fails the suite rather than silently rewriting the baseline.

rules: Do not parallelize critical phases early
Always prefer explicit over implicit behavior
